name: Oats

book_claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
book_claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine

supplementary_research.finding: A 2016 systematic review (Nutr Rev, PMID 26690472) of 58 RCTs confirmed that oat beta-glucan at 3g or more per day significantly reduces LDL and total cholesterol, with a mean LDL reduction of 0.25 mmol/L.
A 2016 meta-analysis (Br J Nutr, PMID 27724985) found oat consumption significantly increased satiety and reduced subsequent energy intake, supporting the weight management benefits of oat fiber.

A 2015 systematic review (Nutr J, PMID 25267243) found oat beta-glucan intake was associated with reduced postprandial glycemic and insulinemic responses, with effects proportional to beta-glucan dose and molecular weight.

A 2019 RCT (Nutrients, PMID 31010505) found that whole oat consumption improved gut microbiota composition and increased short-chain fatty acid production, particularly butyrate, supporting anti-inflammatory effects.

culinary_pairings.source: Granola and muesli tradition; healthy fats complement fiber
Warming spice with complementary blood sugar benefits
Natural binder in oat pancakes and smoothie bowls
Complementary flavonoid profiles in granola and energy bites

seasonality: Year-round pantry staple. Steel-cut (Irish), rolled (old-fashioned), and quick oats differ in processing level and GI. Whole oat groats are least processed.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Soluble fiber forms viscous gel in gut. FDA health claim at 3g/day for cholesterol reduction. Molecular weight and viscosity determine efficacy -- minimally processed oats retain higher MW beta-glucan.
Unique oat polyphenols with anti-inflammatory, anti-itch, and vasodilatory properties. Not found in any other grain.
Cofactor for MnSOD antioxidant enzyme and bone metabolism.
Partially bound by phytic acid. Soaking or fermenting oats improves mineral bioavailability.
Essential for energy metabolism. Well-preserved in minimally processed oats.

synergies.description: Both tested together in cholesterol-lowering food portfolio trial with complementary soluble fibre types
Combined soluble fibre sources for enhanced cholesterol reduction
